title: Nutmegged Fermented Truffles
description: A dense and light Spanish dish featuring fermented truffles with a touch of nutmeg for a whole30-friendly meal.

ingredients.name: fermented mushrooms
ingredients.notes: use a variety of mushrooms, such as oyster or shiitake

ingredients.name: flaxseeds
ingredients.notes: ground them to use in the fermentation process

ingredients.name: nutmeg
ingredients.notes: freshly grated for a stronger flavor

ingredients.name: water
ingredients.notes: to create a brine for fermentation

instructions: Combine water, flaxseeds, and nutmeg in a blender or food processor to make a paste. Set aside.
Sterilize glass jars by boiling them for 10 minutes.
Add the flaxseed-nutmeg paste into each jar, cover with lids, and place on the kitchen counter at room temperature for about 48 hours.
Taste the fermented mixture after 24-48 hours. If it's not sweet enough, let ferment longer or add a small amount of honey (not recommended for whole30).
Once ready, transfer to the refrigerator and store in the fridge.
